task_completion.description: The CSV file containing the 12 survey stations is imported and mapped.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Data Import Wizard being used for 'nile_survey_points.csv', and subsequently, placemarks appearing along the Nile River on the map or listed in the Places panel.

task_completion.description: A folder named 'Nile Corridor Agricultural Survey' is created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a folder named 'Nile Corridor Agricultural Survey' in the Places panel on the left side of the Google Earth Pro window.

task_completion.description: A blue path named 'Nile Main Channel' is drawn tracing the river.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a blue line (width 3) tracing the meanders of the Nile River on the map, and an item named 'Nile Main Channel' inside the 'Nile Corridor Agricultural Survey' folder in the Places panel.

task_completion.description: Two green, semi-transparent polygons are drawn outlining farmland.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for two green, semi-transparent polygons on the map tracing the boundary between green farmland and tan desert. The Places panel should show 'East Bank Agriculture' and 'West Bank Agriculture' inside the created folder.

task_completion.description: The map view is tilted to approximately 60 degrees and a screenshot is saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the map view showing a 3D perspective (tilted, not top-down) and the Google Earth 'Save Image' interface or a file manager/terminal confirming the save to '~/Documents/nile_survey_3d.png'.

task_completion.description: The folder is exported as a KMZ file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Save Place As' dialog box being used to save the 'Nile Corridor Agricultural Survey' folder as 'nile_agricultural_survey.kmz' in the Documents directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the Google Earth Pro GUI to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for interactions with the Google Earth Pro interface (menus, dialogs, drawing tools) rather than the agent writing KML code in a text editor or using a script to generate the files.

integrity.description: The path and polygons appear to be genuinely drawn by hand tracing the features.
integrity.visual_evidence: The blue path and green polygons should have multiple vertices that follow the natural, irregular curves of the river and the farmland boundaries, rather than being simple straight lines or basic geometric shapes.

integrity.description: The agent used the correct application dialogs to save the outputs.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the native Google Earth 'Save Image' and 'Save Place As' dialogs being used to create the final .png and .kmz files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The CSV contains exactly 12 stations. The path 'Nile Main Channel' must trace the river from Aswan High Dam (24.0286°N, 32.8780°E) to Luxor Temple (25.6995°N, 32.6390°E) and be styled blue with width 3. The two polygons must outline irrigated farmland: 'East Bank Agriculture' near Esna (25.29°N, 32.55°E) and 'West Bank Agriculture' near Kom Ombo (24.45°N, 32.93°E), styled green with 50% transparency. The 3D screenshot must be tilted to ~60 degrees and saved to ~/Documents/nile_survey_3d.png. The folder must be exported to ~/Documents/nile_agricultural_survey.kmz.
